# Implementation Readiness Checklist: Device Ingestion

**Purpose**: Validate requirement quality and implementation readiness for the Integration Console ingestion slice before implementation
**Created**: 2026-04-27
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Scope Boundaries

- [x] CHK001 Is the Integration Console requirement clearly scoped as a separate deployable `NetworkMonitoring.IntegrationConsole` worker rather than an extension of the probe? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, Plan §Project Structure]
- [x] CHK002 Are backend/API implementation, persistence, `GET /devices`, UI, login, and RBAC explicitly excluded from this increment across spec, plan, and tasks? [Consistency, Spec §FR-016, Tasks §Scope Guardrails]
- [x] CHK003 Are probe-side discovery and existing Kafka publication changes explicitly excluded so 003 behavior remains stable? [Consistency, Spec §FR-017, Tasks §Scope Guardrails]
- [x] CHK004 Are docker-compose changes framed as optional/reference-only and constrained from altering existing probe/Kafka behavior? [Clarity, Tasks §T052]

## Contract Preservation

- [x] CHK005 Are `DeviceDetected`, `devices.detected`, and `devices.detected-value` consistently described as existing contracts owned by `003-device-discovery`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002–FR-003, Plan §Boundary Contracts]
- [x] CHK006 Is `POST /devices` consistently described as an outbound intake contract for 004, not as a real backend/API implementation? [Clarity, Contracts §device-intake-http, Spec §FR-015–FR-016]
- [x] CHK007 Are key/payload MAC matching requirements clear enough to avoid ambiguous forwarding when identities disagree? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Data Model §Validation Rules]
- [x] CHK008 Is idempotency specified consistently as normalized MAC identity and `Idempotency-Key` across spec, data model, contracts, and tasks? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, Contracts §Headers, Tasks §US4]

## Architecture And Domain Usage

- [x] CHK009 Are Application, Infrastructure, and Host responsibilities described consistently with hexagonal boundaries? [Consistency, Plan §Summary, Tasks §Foundational]
- [x] CHK010 Is shared domain usage limited to validation/normalization value objects without introducing backend business persistence behavior? [Scope, Plan §Constitution Check]
- [x] CHK011 Are SeedWork immutability constraints represented in planning and task coverage? [Constitution, Plan §SeedWork Immutability, Tasks §T017]
- [x] CHK012 Is the separate deployable's Dockerfile requirement documented clearly enough to satisfy the containerized deployable constraint? [Completeness, Plan §Project Structure, Tasks §T007]

## Task Coverage

- [x] CHK013 Do tasks cover Kafka consumption from `devices.detected` and Avro/Schema Registry decoding of `devices.detected-value`? [Coverage, Tasks §US1]
- [x] CHK014 Do tasks cover validation of required event fields, malformed events, key/payload MAC mismatch, and rejected event classification? [Coverage, Tasks §T018–T026]
- [x] CHK015 Do tasks cover HTTP forwarding to configurable `POST /devices` with JSON body and `Idempotency-Key`? [Coverage, Tasks §US2]
- [x] CHK016 Do tasks cover retryable failures, permanent validation failures, retry exhaustion, and poison-message continuation? [Coverage, Tasks §US3]
- [x] CHK017 Do tasks cover duplicate-event and retry idempotency expectations using the fake/test receiver? [Coverage, Tasks §US4]
- [x] CHK018 Do tasks explicitly cover operator-visible diagnostics for consumption, rejection, retry exhaustion, and successful forwarding? [Coverage, Spec §FR-012, Spec §SC-005]

## Acceptance And Validation Readiness

- [x] CHK019 Are all user stories independently testable with fake consumers and/or fake HTTP receiver before the real backend exists? [Testability, Spec §User Scenarios, Tasks §Checkpoints]
- [x] CHK020 Are gated Kafka validation requirements clearly separated from default unit/integration tests via `RUN_KAFKA_INTEGRATION=1`? [Clarity, Tasks §T050/T054, Quickstart §Produce Device Events]
- [x] CHK021 Are fake/test HTTP receiver requirements complete enough to validate request path, body, idempotency header, retry behavior, and duplicate downstream effect? [Completeness, Quickstart §Start Fake Device Receiver]
- [x] CHK022 Are success criteria SC-001 through SC-005 traceable to concrete task groups without requiring the real backend? [Traceability, Spec §Success Criteria, Tasks §US2–Polish]

## Dependencies And Assumptions

- [x] CHK023 Are dependencies on `003-device-discovery`, `005-device-backend`, and `006-device-ui` documented without expanding 004 scope? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK024 Are security requirements stated at the correct level for this non-user-facing worker, with service transport configurable and login/RBAC deferred? [Scope, Plan §Security Controls]
- [x] CHK025 Are processing-position/acknowledgement expectations for succeeded, rejected, and retry-exhausted events specified clearly enough for implementation tasks? [Clarity, Contracts §Consumer Behavior]

## Notes

- Items should be checked against the English requirements and task definitions before `/speckit.implement`.
- CHK018 passed after T032 was clarified to include successful forwarding diagnostics.
- CHK025 passed after T024 was clarified to include processing-position acknowledgement semantics.
